description: All TPM operations (NV read, NV write, hash, sign) use execute_with_nullauth_session (lib.rs:220, 270, 318, 324). This works only if the NV index or key was created with an empty auth value (no password/policy). Production TPM deployments typically set owner auth, NV index auth, or key auth passwords. There is no way to supply an auth value via the URI (unlike PKCS#11's ?pin= parameter). If the NV index or key requires auth, all operations will fail with TPM_RC_AUTH_FAIL. The crate should either: (a) accept ?auth= or ?password= in the URI query string, (b) support an environment variable (e.g. TPM2_AUTH), or (c) document prominently that only null-auth objects are supported.
title: NullAuth session may be insufficient for production NV indices
